keyPair.title: key_pair
keyPair.description: The newly created key pair, including any server-assigned fields.

name.title: name
name.description: The name of the key pair.

certificate.title: certificate
certificate.description: The key pair certificate raw bytes.

examples:
-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----
EXAMPLE
-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----
key.title: key
key.description: The key pair private key raw bytes.

createdAt.title: created_at
createdAt.description: When the key pair was created.

modifiedAt.title: modified_at
modifiedAt.description: When the key pair was last modified.

status.title: status
status.description: The status of the key pair.

origin.title: origin
origin.description: The origin of the key pair.

version.title: version
version.description: X.509 certificate format version (3 for modern certificates).

serial.title: serial
serial.description: Certificate serial number, formatted as a decimal integer string.

issuer.title: issuer
issuer.description: Issuer distinguished name (the CA that signed this certificate).

country.title: country
country.description: Two-letter ISO country codes from the C= relative distinguished name.

serialNumber.title: serial_number
serialNumber.description: X.509 serialNumber attribute of the name (not the certificate serial).

subject.title: subject
subject.description: Subject distinguished name (the identity the certificate vouches for).

country.title: country
country.description: Two-letter ISO country codes from the C= relative distinguished name.

serialNumber.title: serial_number
serialNumber.description: X.509 serialNumber attribute of the name (not the certificate serial).

notBefore.title: not_before
notBefore.description: First instant the certificate is valid.

notAfter.title: not_after
notAfter.description: First instant the certificate is no longer valid.

keyUsage.title: key_usage
keyUsage.description: Decoded KeyUsage and ExtKeyUsage extensions on the certificate.

contentCommitment.title: content_commitment
contentCommitment.description: Certificate authorizes non-repudiation of signed data.

keyEncipherment.title: key_encipherment
keyEncipherment.description: Certificate authorizes key transport (encryption of session keys).

dataEncipherment.title: data_encipherment
dataEncipherment.description: Certificate authorizes direct encryption of arbitrary data.

keyAgreement.title: key_agreement
keyAgreement.description: Certificate authorizes Diffie-Hellman style key agreement.

crlSign.title: crl_sign
crlSign.description: Certificate authorizes signing certificate revocation lists.

encipherOnly.title: encipher_only
encipherOnly.description: With key_agreement, restricts use to encipherment only.

decipherOnly.title: decipher_only
decipherOnly.description: With key_agreement, restricts use to decipherment only.

serverAuth.title: server_auth
serverAuth.description: Extended usage: server authentication (TLS server certificates).

clientAuth.title: client_auth
clientAuth.description: Extended usage: client authentication (TLS client certificates).

dnsNames.title: dns_names
dnsNames.description: DNS names in the Subject Alternative Name extension.

emailAddresses.title: email_addresses
emailAddresses.description: Email addresses in the Subject Alternative Name extension.

ipAddresses.title: ip_addresses
ipAddresses.description:
IP addresses in the Subject Alternative Name extension, formatted as
 strings (IPv4 dotted-decimal or IPv6 colon-hex).

uris.title: uris
uris.description: URIs in the Subject Alternative Name extension.

permittedDnsDomainsCritical.title: permitted_dns_domains_critical
permittedDnsDomainsCritical.description:
When true the permitted/excluded DNS-domain name-constraints are marked
 critical and clients must understand them or reject the certificate.

permittedDnsDomains.title: permitted_dns_domains
permittedDnsDomains.description: Name constraints: DNS domains the certificate is permitted to assert.

excludedDnsDomains.title: excluded_dns_domains
excludedDnsDomains.description: Name constraints: DNS domains the certificate must not assert.

permittedIpRanges.title: permitted_ip_ranges
permittedIpRanges.description: Name constraints: IP-range CIDRs the certificate is permitted to assert.

excludedIpRanges.title: excluded_ip_ranges
excludedIpRanges.description: Name constraints: IP-range CIDRs the certificate must not assert.

permittedEmailAddresses.title: permitted_email_addresses
permittedEmailAddresses.description:
Name constraints: email addresses (or domains) the certificate is
 permitted to assert.

excludedEmailAddresses.title: excluded_email_addresses
excludedEmailAddresses.description:
Name constraints: email addresses (or domains) the certificate must not
 assert.

permittedUriDomains.title: permitted_uri_domains
permittedUriDomains.description: Name constraints: URI domains the certificate is permitted to assert.

excludedUriDomains.title: excluded_uri_domains
excludedUriDomains.description: Name constraints: URI domains the certificate must not assert.

certificateInfo.title: certificate_info
certificateInfo.description: Info about any certificates the key pair has.

code.description: The status code, which should be an enum value of [google.rpc.Code][google.rpc.Code].

message.description: A developer-facing error message, which should be in English. Any user-facing error message should be localized and sent in the [google.rpc.Status.details][google.rpc.Status.details] field, or localized by the client.

type.description: A URL that acts as a globally unique identifier for the type of the serialized message. For example: `type.googleapis.com/google.rpc.ErrorInfo`. This is used to determine the schema of the data in the `value` field and is the discriminator for the `debug` field.

value.description: The Protobuf message, serialized as bytes and base64-encoded. The specific message type is identified by the `type` field.

debug.title: Debug
debug.description: Deserialized error detail payload. The 'type' field indicates the schema. This field is for easier debugging and should not be relied upon for application logic.

items.description: Contains an arbitrary serialized message along with a @type that describes the type of the serialized message, with an additional debug field for ConnectRPC error details.
items.title: connect.error_details.Any

details.description: A list of messages that carry the error details. There is no limit on the number of messages.
